theory.intro: To solve a linear equation of the form $mx+c=k$ from a graph, use the line $y=mx+c$. The solution is the $x$-value of the point where the line reaches the height $y=k$.
facts: Every point $(x,y)$ on the line makes $y=mx+c$ true, so the solution to $mx+c=k$ is the $x$-value where the line's height is $k$.
Draw a horizontal line at $y=k$; its intersection with the graph gives the solution.
To solve $mx+c=0$, read the **$x$-intercept** — where the line crosses the $x$-axis, which is the horizontal line $y=0$.
If the intersection falls between gridlines, read the $x$-value by interpolating (halfway between two whole numbers is $0.5$).
steps: Mark the intersection with the graph.

foundation.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=6cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-2,xmax=4,ymin=-3,ymax=5,xtick={-2,-1,...,4},ytick={-3,-2,...,5},grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,blue!70!black,<->,domain=-1:3,samples=2]{2*x-1} node[pos=0.12,left,text=blue!70!black]{$y=2x-1$};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
The graph of $y=2x-1$ is shown. Use it to solve $2x-1=3$.
foundation.solution_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=6cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-2,xmax=4,ymin=-3,ymax=5,xtick={-2,-1,...,4},ytick={-3,-2,...,5},grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,blue!70!black,<->,domain=-1:3,samples=2]{2*x-1} node[pos=0.12,left,text=blue!70!black]{$y=2x-1$};\addplot[thick,red!75!black,dashed,domain=-2:4,samples=2]{3} node[pos=0.08,above,text=red!75!black]{$y=3$};\draw[dashed] (axis cs:2,3)--(axis cs:2,0);\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=2.5pt,black] coordinates {(2,3)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Draw the horizontal line $y=3$.
It meets $y=2x-1$ at $(2,3)$.
$x=2$

foundation.question_text: To solve $5x+2=12$ using a graph, which line and which horizontal line do you need to draw?
foundation.solution_text:
$5x+2=12$ has the form $mx+c=k$.
Draw the line $y=5x+2$.
Draw the horizontal line $y=12$.

$x=2.5$
